doc.bottomsheet_a11y_backdrop: Ensure the sheet can be closed by tapping the backdrop and support Escape key operations.
doc.bottomsheet_a11y_desc: Ensure the sheet can be closed by tapping the backdrop and support Escape key operations. Use aria-modal and focus trapping.
doc.bottomsheet_anatomy_content: A scrollable area where detailed info or lists of options are placed.
doc.bottomsheet_anatomy_footer: Footer: Contains action buttons like 'Close' or 'Apply'.
doc.bottomsheet_anatomy_handle: Handle: A visual cue indicating that the sheet is draggable.
doc.bottomsheet_anatomy_title: Title: Provides a summary of the sheet's purpose or the information provided.
doc.bottomsheet_best_practice_1: Only use for mobile devices or narrow screens. For desktop, consider using Dialog or Drawer.
doc.bottomsheet_best_practice_2: Display a handle at the top to visually indicate that it can be closed by swiping or dragged.
doc.bottomsheet_best_practice_3: Ideally, keep it simple and focus on a single task or a small number of actions.
doc.bottomsheet_choice_bottomsheet_when: On mobile devices when you want to quickly perform related tasks (sharing, action selection, etc.) without leaving the current screen.
doc.bottomsheet_choice_dialog_when: In desktop environments or when forcing the user to make an urgent and defining decision (e.g., confirming a deletion).
doc.bottomsheet_choice_drawer_when: For desktop side panels (settings, document viewing, etc.) where you want to continue working in parallel with the main content.
doc.bottomsheet_choice_matrix_desc: Select based on screen size and the weight of the task the user is performing.
doc.bottomsheet_content_guideline: Bottom sheets work best for temporary tasks. Avoid stuffing them with complex hierarchical structures or long-duration input forms.
doc.bottomsheet_content_limit: Bottom sheets are best for 'temporary tasks'. Avoid stuffing them with complex hierarchical structures or long-duration input forms.
doc.bottomsheet_desc: Mobile-optimized panel sliding from the bottom of the screen
doc.bottomsheet_design_intent: BottomSheet is a component used in mobile environments to present specific tasks or extra information while maintaining the current context (background screen). By sliding up from the bottom, it provides a user experience optimized for one-handed operation within the reach of the thumb.
doc.bottomsheet_features_backdrop_desc: Darkens the background and blurs content to encourage visual focus.
doc.bottomsheet_features_focus_desc: Has a focus trap feature for accessibility.
doc.bottomsheet_features_handle_desc: Displays a visual handle conforming to common bottom sheet UI.
doc.bottomsheet_features_mobile_opt_desc: Positioned at the bottom, making it easy to reach with the thumb.
doc.bottomsheet_features_scroll_desc: Inner scroll area handles long content.
doc.bottomsheet_long_desc: A bottom sheet is an overlay component fixed to the bottom edge of the screen. Mostly used in mobile interfaces to display task-specific actions or additional info while keeping the current context.
doc.bottomsheet_mobile_only: > Smartphone only: This component is mainly designed for mobile devices. Try Dialog or Drawer for Desktop.
doc.bottomsheet_motion_desc: Slides up smoothly from the bottom and returns downward when closed. The background backdrop appears with a fade.
doc.bottomsheet_motion_slide: Slides up smoothly from the bottom and returns downward when closed. The background backdrop appears with a fade.
doc.bottomsheet_scenario_1: Presenting quick actions like Edit, Delete, or Share when an item is long-pressed.
doc.bottomsheet_scenario_2: Selecting narrowing conditions (categories, sorting) on mobile search result screens.
doc.bottomsheet_scenario_action: Action Selection: Presenting quick actions like 'Edit', 'Delete', or 'Share' when an item is long-pressed.
doc.bottomsheet_scenario_filter: Simple Filtering: Selecting narrowing conditions (categories, sorting) on mobile search result screens.
doc.bottomsheet_title: An overlay panel that slides up from the bottom of the screen, typically used on mobile devices.
doc.commandpalette_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.commandpalette_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.commandpalette_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.commandpalette_best_practice_1: Use CommandPalette consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.commandpalette_best_practice_2: Test CommandPalette behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.commandpalette_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like SearchInput better suits the use case.
doc.commandpalette_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.commandpalette_choice_self_when: When CommandPalette functionality with standard options is required.
doc.commandpalette_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.commandpalette_design_intent: CommandPalette provides a consistent, reusable pattern for keyboard-driven search and command execution overlay, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.commandpalette_feature_1_desc: Instantly open or close the palette with shortcut keys like ⌘K (Ctrl+K).
doc.commandpalette_feature_2_desc: Filter commands, pages, and actions in real-time based on your input.
doc.commandpalette_feature_3_desc: Organize commands into categories using CommandPaletteGroup.
doc.commandpalette_long_desc_1: The Command Palette is a full-screen search UI that can be invoked with keyboard shortcuts like ⌘K.
doc.commandpalette_long_desc_2: Quickly search and execute actions, or navigate pages, entirely via the keyboard.
doc.commandpalette_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.commandpalette_role_1: Root component that manages the overall state of the palette.
doc.commandpalette_role_2: Trigger element that opens the palette.
doc.commandpalette_role_3: Content area where the palette is rendered.
doc.commandpalette_role_4: Text input field for searching commands.
doc.commandpalette_role_5: List that displays filtered search results.
doc.commandpalette_role_6: Container for grouping commands by category.
doc.commandpalette_role_8: Empty state displayed when no results match the search.
doc.commandpalette_role_9: Footer area at the bottom of the palette.
doc.commandpalette_scenario_1: Integrating CommandPalette into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.commandpalette_scenario_2: Using CommandPalette in management views for information organization.
doc.commandpalette_usage_2: Standalone Usage as a Controlled Component
doc.contextmenu_a11y_1: Compliant with WAI-ARIA menu and menuitem roles.
doc.contextmenu_a11y_2: Navigate through menu items using arrow keys.
doc.contextmenu_a11y_3: Close the menu instantly by pressing the Escape key.
doc.contextmenu_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.contextmenu_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.contextmenu_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.contextmenu_best_practice_1: Use ContextMenu consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.contextmenu_best_practice_2: Test ContextMenu behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.contextmenu_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Dropdown better suits the use case.
doc.contextmenu_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.contextmenu_choice_self_when: When ContextMenu functionality with standard options is required.
doc.contextmenu_comp_1_desc: Root component that opens the menu on right-click. Pass menu content via the menu property.
doc.contextmenu_comp_2_desc: Individual menu items. Supports click handlers, icons, and danger states.
doc.contextmenu_comp_3_desc: Divider for visually separating menu items.
doc.contextmenu_comp_4_desc: Container for organizing related menu items into groups.
doc.contextmenu_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.contextmenu_design_intent: ContextMenu provides a consistent, reusable pattern for right-click context-sensitive action menus, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.contextmenu_feat_1_desc: Supports fully customized menu layouts by passing arbitrary JSX.
doc.contextmenu_feat_2_desc: Enhance visibility with icons and clearly mark destructive actions.
doc.contextmenu_feat_3_desc: Easily disable any item or the entire menu based on permissions.
doc.contextmenu_feat_4_desc: Keep complex menus organized and easy to scan.
doc.contextmenu_long_desc_1: A component for showing context-sensitive menus on right-click. Useful for secondary actions like file operations.
doc.contextmenu_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.contextmenu_note: Right-click context menus may not be fully supported on mobile. Consider using BottomSheet as an alternative.
doc.contextmenu_prop_1_desc: Specifies the menu content as JSX.
doc.contextmenu_prop_2_desc: Disables the context menu, preventing it from appearing on right-click.
doc.contextmenu_prop_3_desc: Icon displayed on the left side of the item.
doc.contextmenu_prop_4_desc: Applies danger styling (red text) for destructive actions like 'Delete'.
doc.contextmenu_prop_5_desc: Disables specific menu items, making them unclickable.
doc.contextmenu_scenario_1: Integrating ContextMenu into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.contextmenu_scenario_2: Using ContextMenu in management views for information organization.
doc.contextmenu_story_1_desc: An example displaying menu items with associated icons.
doc.contextmenu_story_2_desc: Organizing items into logical categories using ContextMenuGroup.
doc.contextmenu_story_3_desc: Example showing how to display menu items in a disabled state.
doc.contextmenu_story_4_desc: Custom menu displayed when right-clicking on an image.
doc.dialog_a11y_1: Correctly manages focus on open/close (Focus Trapping).
doc.dialog_a11y_2: Root element uses role='dialog' or role='alertdialog'.
doc.dialog_a11y_3: Linked to titles and descriptions via aria-labelledby and aria-describedby.
doc.dialog_a11y_4: Supports closing the dialog via the Escape key.
doc.dialog_a11y_trap: Enables a focus trap to keep keyboard navigation within the dialog, ensuring accessibility for assistive technology users.
doc.dialog_accessibility_gaze_desc: When a dialog opens, focus automatically moves inside the dialog, and when it closes, it returns to the previously active element.
doc.dialog_alert_dialog_desc: Use for critical notifications requiring user confirmation (e.g., confirming a deletion).
doc.dialog_anatomy_backdrop: Dims the background to isolate the user's focus on the dialog.
doc.dialog_anatomy_footer: Contains action buttons like 'Save' or 'Cancel', typically aligned in a row on the right.
doc.dialog_anatomy_header: Contains the title to communicate the purpose and an optional description.
doc.dialog_basic_desc: Detailed information and usage instructions for the Dialog component.
doc.dialog_basic_usage_desc: Standard implementation of the Dialog component, triggered by a button or other interaction.
doc.dialog_best_practice_1: Keep actions within the dialog clear and use predictable labels (e.g., Save/Discard instead of OK/Cancel).
doc.dialog_best_practice_2: Avoid displaying another dialog inside a dialog (nesting), as it confuses users.
doc.dialog_choice_dialog_when: For critical confirmations (e.g., deletion) or focused form inputs where a clear decision is required.
doc.dialog_choice_drawer_when: For browsing rich content or complex settings that require more vertical screen space.
doc.dialog_choice_matrix_desc: Choose based on the complexity of the information and the degree of interruption required.
doc.dialog_choice_popover_when: For lightweight settings or contextual inputs where staying close to the trigger element is preferred.
doc.dialog_content_button_labels: Use descriptive verbs for button labels (e.g., 'Save', 'Delete') so users know exactly what will happen.
doc.dialog_controlled_desc: Example of managing the open state using an external value.
doc.dialog_custom_content_desc: Inject arbitrary JSX into the dialog body for complex forms or information displays.
doc.dialog_design_intent: Dialogs are interactive windows used to prompt for mandatory user action, provide information that requires a decision, or perform critical tasks. They aim to focus the user's attention on a specific task or confirmation without losing the current screen context.
doc.dialog_design_philosophy_desc: Principles of interaction design centered on clear user intent.
doc.dialog_eliminate_ambiguity_desc: Ensure the user knows exactly what action the dialog will take before they confirm.
doc.dialog_guideline_1: Use when you need to ask for confirmation, prompt for important input, or interrupt the user's task to demand attention.
doc.dialog_guideline_2: When displaying a modal, darken the background and disable interactions behind it to focus the user's attention on the current action.
doc.dialog_long_desc: Details and usage for the Dialog component.
doc.dialog_motion_scale: Applies a subtle scale-up and fade-in animation to create a sense of depth and focus when the dialog appears.
doc.dialog_ooui_consistency_desc: Maintain consistency with Object-Oriented User Interface principles for intuitive behavior.
doc.dialog_scenario_confirm: Action Confirmation: 'Are you sure you want to delete this project?' before permanent actions.
doc.dialog_scenario_form: Data Entry: Focused tasks like adding a new contact or updating account settings.
doc.dialog_size_variations_desc: Control the dialog width using properties like size='sm', 'md', 'lg', or 'xl'.
doc.dialog_uncontrolled_desc: Example where the component manages its own open state internally.
doc.dialog_why_not_modal_desc: 'Modal' is an adjective describing a state where interaction is restricted. By using the proper name 'Dialog', we more accurately represent the UI component.
doc.dialog_with_header_footer_desc: Structure complex dialogs using Dialog.Header (for titles) and Dialog.Footer (for primary actions).
doc.drawer_a11y_escape: Ensure it can be closed with the Escape key and that the background content is not inadvertently announced while the drawer is open.
doc.drawer_anatomy_header: Features a title and a clear 'X' button to dismiss the panel.
doc.drawer_anatomy_overlay: A background mask that restricts interaction with the underlying content to focus on the drawer.
doc.drawer_anatomy_panel: The content area emerging from a screen edge (right, left, top, bottom). Usually scrollable.
doc.drawer_basic_principles_desc: Core guidelines for using drawers effectively in complex workflows.
doc.drawer_basic_usage_desc: Standard implementation of the Drawer component for side-out panels.
doc.drawer_best_practice_1: Use when significant space is needed for detailed information while maintaining the context of the current task.
doc.drawer_best_practice_2: Best for scenarios where users need to frequently refer to information or compare multiple items.
doc.drawer_best_practice_3: Always provide a clear title for the content being displayed and an easy way to close the drawer.
doc.drawer_choice_bottomsheet_when: For mobile experiences where info should emerge from the thumb-friendly bottom of the screen.
doc.drawer_choice_dialog_when: For short, focused interactions like 'Confirm deletion?' that require a decisive user action.
doc.drawer_choice_drawer_when: For scenarios where users need to read long content comfortably, such as 'Email Detail View' or 'Property Spec Comparison'.
doc.drawer_choice_matrix_desc: Select based on the volume of information to display and the frequency of interaction.
doc.drawer_content_hierarchy: Consider information priority within the drawer; for example, fixing important action buttons near the footer for better usability.
doc.drawer_controlled_desc: Managing the open/close state from parent components for custom logic.
doc.drawer_design_intent: Drawers are sliding panels used to present large amounts of additional information or complex control panels while maintaining the context of the main screen. They offer more vertical space than Dialogs, making them ideal for detailed data views or repeatable operations like filter settings.
doc.drawer_long_desc: Detailed information and usage instructions for the Drawer component.
doc.drawer_motion_glide: Uses a 'Glide-in' animation sliding smoothly from a screen edge to physically represent its point of origin.
doc.drawer_note_main_action_desc: Keep primary actions reachable within the drawer content area.
doc.drawer_note_mobile_desc: Adaptive design considerations for small-screen interactions.
doc.drawer_scenario_detail: Detail Viewing: Displaying specs or deep-dive info when a list item is clicked.
doc.drawer_scenario_filter: Advanced Filtering: Using a panel to contain a multitude of search criteria or settings.
doc.drawer_scene_desktop_desc: Typical usage patterns for side-panels in desktop applications.
doc.drawer_scene_future_desc: Advanced interaction patterns for upcoming complex feature sets.
doc.drawer_scene_multi_level_desc: Suitable for layered navigation such as category side menus and settings screens.
doc.drawer_type_modal_desc: A modal drawer that provides a backdrop and focuses user attention.
doc.drawer_type_standard_desc: Displayed in parallel with the main content without a backdrop. Suitable for desktop side panels.
doc.dropdown_a11y_1: Fully navigable via keyboard (arrow keys, Enter, Escape).
doc.dropdown_a11y_2: Appropriate ARIA roles (menu, menuitem) ensure screen reader compatibility.
doc.dropdown_a11y_3: Maintains correct focus states when opening and closing.
doc.dropdown_a11y_4: Selection states are clearly communicated using aria-checked.
doc.dropdown_a11y_spec: Navigate between items using the up and down arrow keys, and select using the Enter key. Close the menu with the Escape key. Roles and attributes are appropriately controlled.
doc.dropdown_anatomy_item: Individual actions. Can include text, icons, and subtext.
doc.dropdown_anatomy_menu: A floating panel that holds the list of items.
doc.dropdown_anatomy_trigger: A button or avatar used to expand the dropdown.
doc.dropdown_api_checkbox_item_desc: Menu items that toggle a checked state.
doc.dropdown_api_content_desc: The floating container where menu items are rendered.
doc.dropdown_api_dropdown: Dropdown: The root component managing the overall state of the Dropdown.
doc.dropdown_api_group_desc: Logical grouping of menu items with an optional label.
doc.dropdown_api_label_desc: A non-clickable header label for a group.
doc.dropdown_api_menu: Dropdown.Menu: A container for menu items.
doc.dropdown_api_radio_group_desc: Container for a set of mutually exclusive radio items.
doc.dropdown_api_root_desc: Root component that manages the overall dropdown state.
doc.dropdown_api_separator_desc: A horizontal line used to visually separate items.
doc.dropdown_api_sub_content_desc: The floating container for sub-menu items.
doc.dropdown_api_sub_trigger_desc: The item that opens a nested submenu.
doc.dropdown_api_trigger_desc: The button or element that toggles the dropdown visibility.
doc.dropdown_basic_usage_desc: Standard implementation of the Dropdown component, commonly used for menus and settings.
doc.dropdown_bp_access_desc: Follow the WAI-ARIA Listbox pattern to ensure it can be opened and closed via keyboard.
doc.dropdown_bp_feedback_desc: Display the current selection on the trigger button to provide clear feedback to the user.
doc.dropdown_bp_layout_desc: Ensure the menu width is at least as wide as the trigger, and consider position adjustments to prevent it from being cut off at screen edges.
doc.dropdown_checkbox_radio_desc: Include checkboxes or radio buttons within the dropdown to manage state selections.
doc.dropdown_choice_dropdown_when: When you want to display a list of actions (e.g., 'Copy', 'Delete').
doc.dropdown_choice_matrix_desc: Choose based on the context and the complexity of the information required.
doc.dropdown_choice_popover_when: When you want to display supplementary information or more complex content (e.g., a form).
doc.dropdown_choice_selectbox_when: When selecting settings or values (e.g., 'State') within a form.
doc.dropdown_con_cost_desc: Requires at least two steps (click to open and select), resulting in higher operational cost than radio buttons.
doc.dropdown_con_visibility_desc: Since options are hidden by default, it may be difficult for users to confirm the selected state at a glance.
doc.dropdown_content_guidelines: Menu items should be concise nouns or verbs (e.g., 'Profile', 'Logout'). Use visual distinctions, such as red color, for destructive actions (e.g., 'Delete').
doc.dropdown_criteria_few_reason: For very few options, consider using SegmentedControl or RadioGroup for better visibility.
doc.dropdown_criteria_free_reason: If users need to enter custom text, consider using Autocomplete or Combobox.
doc.dropdown_criteria_many_reason: Dropdowns are ideal for moderate sets; use search for sets larger than 15 items.
doc.dropdown_criteria_multiple_reason: Use CheckboxGroup or MultiSelect when users need to pick more than one item.
doc.dropdown_design_intent: The Dropdown component displays a list of actions or choices in an overlay when a trigger, such as a button, is clicked. It is intended to provide access to multiple related functions within a limited space.
doc.dropdown_grouping_desc: Organize related actions into categories using Dropdown.Group and labels.
doc.dropdown_long_desc: Details and usage for the Dropdown component.
doc.dropdown_motion_spec: When the trigger is clicked, the panel expands with a subtle scale-up and fade-in animation.
doc.dropdown_principle_1_desc: Use short, action-oriented labels for each menu item.
doc.dropdown_principle_2_desc: Place related actions together and use dividers to separate functional blocks.
doc.dropdown_principle_3_desc: Use icons to help users scan and identify actions more quickly.
doc.dropdown_principles_desc: Follow these guidelines to ensure a consistent and user-friendly experience.
doc.dropdown_pro_convenience_desc: Even with many options, it can be displayed compactly, reducing the user's operational load.
doc.dropdown_pro_data_desc: The same UI can handle both few and many options. It is suitable for cases where content changes dynamically.
doc.dropdown_pro_error_desc: Selecting from a list prevents formatting errors caused by manual user input.
doc.dropdown_pro_space_desc: Since options are displayed in a popup, screen space can be used effectively.
doc.dropdown_right_alignment_desc: Example of displaying the menu aligned to the right edge of the trigger.
doc.dropdown_scenario_admin: Table Operations: Providing combined operations like downloading data, editing, or viewing details for each row.
doc.dropdown_scenario_collab: File Sharing: Presenting actions like 'Rename', 'Copy link', 'Share', and 'Delete' from a '...' button next to the file name.
doc.dropdown_scenario_nav: User Settings: Displaying 'My Profile', 'Account Settings', and 'Logout' from the user icon in the header.
doc.dropdown_submenus_desc: Create hierarchical navigation by nesting menus within a single dropdown.
doc.dropdown_vs_checkbox_dd_desc: Multi-select Dropdowns are suitable for multiple selections with many options.
doc.dropdown_vs_checkbox_desc: Suitable for multiple selections with a small number of options.
doc.dropdown_vs_combobox_dd_desc: A simple selection list for choosing from options only.
doc.dropdown_vs_combobox_desc: Supports combined input and selection, including incremental search.
doc.dropdown_vs_multiselect_dd_desc: Suitable for multiple selections where search is not required.
doc.dropdown_vs_multiselect_desc: Integrates multiple selection with search and tag display.
doc.dropdown_vs_radio_dd_desc: Suitable when there are many options or to save screen space.
doc.dropdown_vs_radio_desc: Suitable when there are few options and you want them always visible.
doc.dropdown_with_icons_desc: Enhance navigation by adding icons to the left side of menu items.
doc.hamburgermenu_long_desc: The HamburgerMenu is a component for storing navigation items, primarily for mobile devices.
doc.hamburgermenu_mobile_only_desc: This component is primarily designed for mobile devices (narrow screen widths). For desktop environments, consider using Navbar or Sidebar.
doc.hamburgermenu_title: Collapsible navigation menu for mobile devices
doc.hovercard_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.hovercard_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.hovercard_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.hovercard_best_practice_1: Use HoverCard consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.hovercard_best_practice_2: Test HoverCard behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.hovercard_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Tooltip better suits the use case.
doc.hovercard_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.hovercard_choice_self_when: When HoverCard functionality with standard options is required.
doc.hovercard_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.hovercard_desc: Card that displays detailed information on hover
doc.hovercard_design_intent: HoverCard provides a consistent, reusable pattern for rich content preview card shown on hover, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.hovercard_feat_complexity_desc: Tooltips handle only short text, while HoverCards can handle rich content (images, links, multiple paragraphs, etc.).
doc.hovercard_feat_delay_desc: To prevent accidental triggers, a longer display delay (default 700ms) is typically set compared to Tooltips.
doc.hovercard_feat_interaction_desc: HoverCards are designed for hovering over the displayed content itself or clicking links within it.
doc.hovercard_feat_interaction_rich: Enhanced interactivity including links, media, and nested buttons.
doc.hovercard_long_desc: A component to display additional information when hovered.
doc.hovercard_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.hovercard_pc_only_desc: This component is designed primarily for PCs as it assumes pointer hover operations. It may not work as intended on touch devices.
doc.hovercard_scenario_1: Integrating HoverCard into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.hovercard_scenario_2: Using HoverCard in management views for information organization.
doc.hovercard_title: A floating card that reveals additional information or previews when hovering over an element.
doc.hovercard_vs_tooltip: HoverCard is similar to Tooltip, but differs in the following ways:
doc.menu_a11y_1: Keyboard navigation support (focus moves with Tab key)
doc.menu_comp_divider_desc: Inserts a divider between menu items.
doc.menu_comp_itemgroup_desc: Groups menu items with a displayed title.
doc.menu_comp_menu_desc: Root component managing display modes and open/closed states.
doc.menu_comp_menuitem_desc: Individual menu items. Supports click events and icons.
doc.menu_comp_submenu_desc: Expandable submenus. Requires a unique itemKey.
doc.menu_long_desc: A versatile menu component for navigation or action lists. Supports submenus, grouping, and multiple display modes.
doc.menu_props_default_open_keys: Array of keys for submenu opened by default
doc.menu_stories_groups_desc: Example of grouping menu items, also using dividers.
doc.menu_stories_horizontal_desc: Horizontal menu suitable for header navigation.
doc.menu_stories_icons_desc: Example of menu items with icons.
doc.menu_stories_inline_desc: Inline mode menu suitable for sidebar navigation.
doc.menu_stories_submenu_desc: Example of a menu with submenus. Submenus expand/collapse on click.
doc.popconfirm_best_practice_1: Use for irreversible or high-impact actions (delete, discard) where simple confirmation is needed.
doc.popconfirm_best_practice_2: Keep the confirmation message short and make the destructive action button clearly distinguishable.
doc.popover_a11y_focus: It is recommended to move focus to the first input field when the popover opens and trap focus within the overlay.
doc.popover_anatomy_arrow: Visually points to the element from which the popover originated (optional).
doc.popover_anatomy_content: The floating container that houses forms, lists, or rich text.
doc.popover_anatomy_trigger: The element that invokes the popover upon clicking.
doc.popover_best_practice_1: Use for displaying supplementary information or simple input forms without a full page transition.
doc.popover_best_practice_2: Make the trigger element clear and ensure the popover can be easily dismissed by clicking outside or via an explicit close button.
doc.popover_best_practice_3: Keep the content within the popover focused; consider using a Dialog for more complex layouts or multi-step processes.
doc.popover_choice_dialog_when: For critical decisions or large-scale forms that span multiple steps.
doc.popover_choice_matrix_desc: Select based on the complexity of information and the requirement for task completion.
doc.popover_choice_popover_when: For filter settings related to a specific button or when simple form inputs are required.
doc.popover_choice_tooltip_when: For purely textual descriptions that require no user interaction.
doc.popover_content_compact: Keep the layout inside popovers simple; consider moving to a Dialog for content so long it requires extensive scrolling.
doc.popover_design_intent: Popovers are interactive overlays used to provide detailed information or additional actions related to a specific element while maintaining the current screen context. They are less intrusive than Dialogs and more functional than Tooltips, allowing for complex operations like inputs, selections, or link clicks.
doc.popover_feat_interaction_complex: Supports focus trapping and complex nested components like forms.
doc.popover_long_desc: Detailed information and usage instructions for the Popover component.
doc.popover_motion_slide: Applies a subtle slide-in and fade-in from the trigger element to emphasize the connection between information.
doc.popover_note: Use Popover only when interactive content is required. For simple supplementary explanations, use Tooltip instead.
doc.popover_scenario_filter: Data Filters: A UI for setting detailed filtering conditions in table columns.
doc.popover_scenario_settings: Quick Settings: User preferences when clicking an avatar or status change tools.
doc.popover_use_cases: Filter panels, inline settings, further action menus, etc.
doc.tooltip_a11y_linkage: Uses aria-describedby to link the trigger and tooltip, allowing screen readers to announce the supplementary information.
doc.tooltip_anatomy_content: A small balloon with a dark background providing concise hints or labels.
doc.tooltip_anatomy_trigger: The element (button, icon, etc.) that reveals the tooltip on hover or focus.
doc.tooltip_best_practice_1: Use for displaying supplementary explanations for icons or short text.
doc.tooltip_best_practice_2: Do not include interactive elements like links or buttons within a tooltip. Use a Popover for that instead.
doc.tooltip_best_practice_3: Keep text concise and ensure that important information can be understood even without the tooltip.
doc.tooltip_choice_hovercard_when: For rich previews like user profiles or link metadata that include images or multiple sections.
doc.tooltip_choice_matrix_desc: Select based on the amount of content and the level of interaction required.
doc.tooltip_choice_popover_when: For richer content like links, buttons, or longer descriptions that might need interaction.
doc.tooltip_choice_tooltip_when: For short, plain text descriptions that require no user interaction beyond hovering.
doc.tooltip_content_length: Keep tooltip text extremely concise (ideally 3-5 words) to ensure it can be read at a glance.
doc.tooltip_delay_desc: You can set the delay time before the tooltip is displayed using the `delay` property (Default: 200ms).
doc.tooltip_design_intent: Tooltips are lightweight text labels that provide extra information about an interactive element (like icon-only buttons) or clarify technical terms. They help prevent user confusion and lower the learning curve without cluttering the UI.
doc.tooltip_long_desc: Tooltips are used to provide context and explanations for UI elements, supplementing information without interrupting user actions.
doc.tooltip_motion_delay: Introduces a short delay (around 200ms) before appearing to avoid accidental triggers during mouse movement.
doc.tooltip_scenario_help: Glossary: Defining technical terms or abbreviations in context.
doc.tooltip_scenario_icon: Icon Labels: Providing text labels for icon-only buttons like '+' or '?'.
doc.tour_desc: Sequential guided walkthroughs to onboard users or highlight features
doc.tour_feature_desc: `Tour` displays step-by-step instructions while highlighting specific elements. Ideal for onboarding new users or introducing new features.
doc.tour_long_desc: A guide component to walk users through features or screen operations.
doc.tour_title: Sequential guided walkthroughs to onboard users or highlight features
doc.tour_usage_desc: Specify element selectors, titles, and descriptions for each step in the `steps` prop. Control the tour visibility with the `open` prop.
doc.bottomsheet_keyboard_desc: Tab moves focus within the bottom sheet. Escape closes it. Focus is trapped inside while open.
doc.bottomsheet_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the title and action label props.
doc.contextmenu_keyboard_desc: Arrow keys navigate menu items. Enter or Space activates the focused item. Escape closes the menu.
doc.contextmenu_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the menu item label props.
doc.dialog_keyboard_desc: Tab moves focus within the dialog. Escape closes it. Focus is trapped inside while open.
doc.dialog_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the title and content props.
doc.drawer_keyboard_desc: Tab moves focus within the drawer. Escape closes it. Focus is trapped inside while open.
doc.drawer_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the title and content props.
doc.dropzone_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the dropzone. Enter or Space opens a file picker. Keyboard activation is supported as an alternative to drag-and-drop.
doc.dropzone_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the label and helper text props.
doc.loader_keyboard_desc: This component does not require specific keyboard interaction beyond standard Tab focus.
doc.loader_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the aria-label prop.
doc.menu_keyboard_desc: Arrow keys navigate menu items. Enter or Space activates the focused item. Escape closes submenus or the menu.
doc.menu_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through menu item labels and the aria-label prop.
doc.popover_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the trigger element. Enter or Space opens the popover. Tab moves focus within it. Escape closes it.
doc.popover_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the content and aria-label props.
doc.commandpalette_keyboard_desc: Type to search commands. Arrow keys navigate the results list. Enter activates the focused command. Escape closes the command palette.
doc.commandpalette_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the placeholder, group label, and item label props.
doc.hovercard_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the trigger element. The hover card appears when the trigger receives focus. Escape closes the card. Focus is not trapped inside the card.
doc.hovercard_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the card content children.

overlay.bottomsheet_mobile_desc: BottomSheet emerging from the bottom is best for one-handed operation. Be careful as Dialogs can be hidden by the keyboard if there are many inputs.
overlay.bottomsheet_usage: Mobile action selection, item details, settings.
overlay.cmd_desc: Search and execute commands or menus.
overlay.context_menu_usage: File operations, shortcut menus per element.
overlay.context_supp_desc: Presents supplementary information while maintaining context.
overlay.dialog_usage: Critical confirmations, complex forms, preventing fatal operations.
overlay.drawer_desktop_desc: For large amounts of info, Drawer (Side panel) provides high visibility and ease of operation.
overlay.drawer_usage: Nav menus, viewing detailed info, setting panels.
overlay.float_trigger: Floats right next to the trigger.
overlay.guide: Guidelines for selecting the optimal overlay depending on the UI's purpose.
overlay.guide_flow_desc: Guides through a series of operations.
overlay.hover_rich: Provides images, links, or buttons upon hover.
overlay.instant_action: Instantly presents operations related to the current element.
overlay.long_content_desc: Ideal for vertically long content or settings.
overlay.modal_blocking_desc: Prevents interaction with the background screen.
overlay.modal_usage_criteria: When you want to focus on a task or prevent critical mistakes.
overlay.modeless_blocking_desc: Allows interaction with the background screen while displayed.
overlay.modeless_usage_criteria: When you want to continue work while referring to the original screen.
overlay.popover_usage: Supplementary info, quick settings, context menus.
overlay.sidebar_ref: Allows secondary actions while referring to the original screen.
overlay.swipe_close: Appears within easy reach and can be swiped to close.
overlay.tooltip_desc: Conveys simple meaning of the UI upon hover, etc. (Non-interactive).
overlay.tour_desc: Sequentially highlights specific elements to teach their usage.
overlay.tour_usage: Onboarding, new feature announcements, operation guides.
